summary: No-ship: DS2 still has exact-session identity loss, state write races, and unsafe resume command construction.

findings.title: Exact-session identity still collapses to cwd/branch
findings.recommendation: Require a non-empty validated Claude session id for /sessions/open, upsert by claudeSessionId rather than cwd plus branch, and remove or tightly gate the cwd-based isOpen fallback. Add coverage for two Claude sessions in the same cwd and branch; the second must create/open a distinct tab and resume its own id.

findings.title: Concurrent state writes can lose unpins or corrupt state.json
findings.recommendation: Serialize all state.json mutations through a single writer or file lock, use unique temp files for atomic writes, and merge against the latest state before writing. Add a regression that races close/unpin with a hook status update and proves openedInDevEnv remains false.

findings.title: resumeId is shell-injectable when lazy resume writes claude command
findings.recommendation: Do not use JSON.stringify as shell escaping. Validate resume ids against the expected Claude session id format and quote with a real shell-escape helper, or avoid the shell string path and spawn claude with argv. Add tests with $(), backticks, quotes, and spaces.

findings.title: Worktree-created tabs are not pinned into the reboot-durable open set
findings.recommendation: After createWorktree creates the session record, call setSessionOpen(created.id, true) before assembling the response, unless createWorktree is changed to set openedInDevEnv itself. Add reboot-restore coverage for POST /worktrees-created sessions.
